title: buildah rmi issue fails on private repository using image id
body:
When an image is created on a private repository in docker.io and an image is created from it, you can not remove the image using buildah by passing the imageID to the 'buildah rmi' command.  However it does succeed if you use the Image Name of the image rather than its ID.  Test snippet below.

# buildah images
IMAGE ID     IMAGE NAME
IMAGE ID             IMAGE NAME                                               CREATED AT             SIZE
2a6c6a8e8a0c         docker.io/library/fedora:latest                          Jun 30, 2017 19:24     220.2 MB
a222d5fe42fc         docker.io/tomsweeneyredhat/testing:first                 Jul 13, 2017 19:16     244.8 MB

# buildah rmi a222d5fe42fc
ERRO[0000] reference "[overlay@/var/lib/containers/storage]docker.io/library/a222d5fe42fc:latest" does not resolve to an image ID 
ERRO[0000] could not get image "a222d5fe42fc": error parsing "@a222d5fe42fc" as a storage image reference: invalid checksum digest length 

# buildah rmi docker.io/tomsweeneyredhat/testing:first
untagged: docker.io/tomsweeneyredhat/testing:first
a222d5fe42fc72bdd5d334d247e32254b63c29edea4427a8b2ee7e8983495407

# buildah rmi docker.io/tomsweeneyredhat/testing:first
untagged: docker.io/tomsweeneyredhat/testing:first
a222d5fe42fc72bdd5d334d247e32254b63c29edea4427a8b2ee7e8983495407

comments.body: Tom have you investigated why this happens?
Still happening.  I talked to Nalin about it and was under the impression that he'd a handle on it so I didn't poke further.  I can take a look if you @nalind would like.
Most of our handling of truncated IDs should get fixed by updates to containers/image that let it recognize images by truncated ID, but we can work around that not being there yet in #211.
@TomSweeneyRedHat Looks like this is fixed.  Ok to close?

This error has gotten slightly better.  Now I'm just seeing the first error, but the image is getting removed.

```
[root@localhost buildah]# buildah images
IMAGE ID             IMAGE NAME                                               CREATED AT             SIZE
449c3bbf3106         docker.io/kubernetes/pause:latest                        Jul 19, 2014 07:02     241 KB
67cbc7d7ad9f                                                                  Sep 21, 2017 21:01     26.23 MB
67cbc7d7ad9f         <none>                                                   Sep 21, 2017 21:01     26.23 MB
e12e873ea4d7         docker.io/library/redis:alpine                           Sep 21, 2017 21:01     26.23 MB
5d393c629a58         docker.io/library/fedora:latest                          Sep 15, 2017 23:28     220.4 MB
3468107a4544                                                                  Sep 13, 2017 14:32     3.784 MB
3468107a4544         <none>                                                   Sep 13, 2017 14:32     3.784 MB

992c9f17da52         <none>                                                   Sep 13, 2017 14:32     3.784 MB
ef6f7979f0e2         docker.io/library/alpine:latest                          Sep 13, 2017 14:32     3.784 MB
d65df6a58621         docker.io/library/fedora:25                              Sep 15, 2017 23:28     221.7 MB
d4187eb83f07         docker.io/library/fedora:26                              Sep 15, 2017 23:28     220.4 MB
474787f66921         docker.io/tomsweeneyredhat/testing:first                 Jul 12, 2017 16:57     244.8 MB

[root@localhost buildah]# buildah rmi 474787f66921
ERRO[0000] reference "[overlay@/var/lib/containers/storage+/var/run/containers/storage]docker.io/library/474787f66921:latest" does not resolve to an image ID 
474787f66921d65424b1fe47c886fc6995135ab417bf82d8501120be22822d18

But he image is removed despite the error.
[root@localhost buildah]# buildah images
IMAGE ID             IMAGE NAME                                               CREATED AT             SIZE
449c3bbf3106         docker.io/kubernetes/pause:latest                        Jul 19, 2014 07:02     241 KB
67cbc7d7ad9f                                                                  Sep 21, 2017 21:01     26.23 MB
67cbc7d7ad9f         <none>                                                   Sep 21, 2017 21:01     26.23 MB

3468107a4544                                                                  Sep 13, 2017 14:32     3.784 MB
3468107a4544         <none>                                                   Sep 13, 2017 14:32     3.784 MB
94c325a8c1ad                                                                  Sep 13, 2017 14:32     3.784 MB
94c325a8c1ad         <none>                                                   Sep 13, 2017 14:32     3.784 MB
992c9f17da52                                                                  Sep 13, 2017 14:32     3.784 MB
992c9f17da52         <none>                                                   Sep 13, 2017 14:32     3.784 MB
ef6f7979f0e2         docker.io/library/alpine:latest                          Sep 13, 2017 14:32     3.784 MB
d65df6a58621         docker.io/library/fedora:25                              Sep 15, 2017 23:28     221.7 MB
d4187eb83f07         docker.io/library/fedora:26                              Sep 15, 2017 23:28     220.4 MB
```

I'd say keep it open for now.

I've just retested this issue and can no longer reproduce it or it's latest variant.  I'm sure one or more of the image handling changes that @nalind has put into play over the past several months/weeks has cleaned this up finally.
